subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Identify line symmetry in regular and irregular shapes
Understand the concept of the 'Axis of Symmetry'
Recognize symmetrical patterns in nature and architecture
Draw the other half of a symmetrical figure given the axis

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Line Symmetry
topics_covered: Definition of symmetry and folding test
Identifying lines of symmetry in letters (A, B, M, etc.)
student_activities: Fold paper and cut out symmetrical shapes (hearts, butterflies)
Identify which capital letters have more than one line of symmetry
assessment: Oral quiz: 'How many lines of symmetry in a rectangle?'
Worksheet on completing the mirror image

lesson_plan.title: Reflection and Symmetry
student_activities: Use a strip of mirror to find the axis of symmetry in pictures
Draw the reflection of simple shapes on a square grid

teacher_tip: Remind students that a 'line of symmetry' is like a crease—if you can't fold it perfectly over that line, it isn't one!
